notes: Collection-only run for the next Taiwan trading day background layer.

The latest official TWSE cash-session and institutional-flow data available at capture time are for 2026-04-21.

notes: Market driver summary is background-only and does not pretend to reconstruct a full machine-readable news layer.

trading_day_reference.description: 2026-04-22 is not listed as a TWSE holiday in the official 2026 schedule and is treated as a normal trading day.

derived_calendar_notes: 2026-04-22 follows the 2026-04-21 cash session and is the next regular TWSE trading day.
This is an overnight/pre-open background build; same-day 2026-04-22 official close and institutional-flow are not yet available.

notes: Latest official TWSE cash-session close available prior to the 2026-04-22 pre-open context build.
Technology-heavy leadership remained visible: Electronics +2.13%, Semiconductor +1.94%, Computer and Peripheral Equipment +2.70%, Electronic Parts/Components +3.80%.
Lagging groups included Finance and Insurance -0.26% and Oil, Gas and Electricity -1.60%.
Official three-institution trading value snapshot for 2026-04-21 showed strong broad buy-side support.
Foreign investors were net buyers above NT$60bn, while investment trusts were net buyers above NT$8bn; this is constructive tape background but not itself a next-day thesis conclusion.

last_session_leadership.interpretation: The most recent official session showed capital preference tilted toward large electronics, semiconductors, AI-adjacent hardware, and component groups rather than defensives or energy-linked pockets.

secondary_drivers: Last-known Taiwan cash session showed strong TAIEX upside with technology-heavy sector leadership
Foreign investors and investment trusts both posted sizeable net buying on 2026-04-21
Without the intended news_digest input, macro/news catalyst ranking remains incomplete and should not be over-inferred
likely_affected_groups: AI-adjacent hardware, server, component, and communications supply-chain groups
Broad Taiwan tech ETFs or index products more exposed to electronic weighting
Relatively less immediate support visible for finance, energy-sensitive, and defensive pockets in the last known session
market_driver_summary.why_it_matters: The last official Taiwan session provides usable tape context: strong index rebound, heavy foreign participation, and clear technology leadership. That is valuable as background for downstream thesis work, but the intended machine-readable message layer is missing, so this run should not pretend to know which macro or geopolitical headline truly dominated the coming session.
market_driver_summary.what_would_invalidate: If the missing news digest later reveals a different dominant catalyst, or if overnight macro/geopolitical developments materially changed risk appetite after the 2026-04-21 close, this background-only framing could become incomplete.
